text: MSME Samadhaan (Delayed Payment Monitoring System) Purpose: A portal for MSEs to file grievances against buyers for delayed payments. Eligibility: Any micro or small enterprise with a valid Udyam Registration. Benefits: MSEs can claim payments due including compound interest (3x bank rate) if not paid within 45 days. Cases are reviewed by MSEFC. Application: Online via Samadhaan portal (https://samadhaan.msme.gov.in/) using Udyam/UAM and OTP.

Export Promotion Capital Goods (EPCG) Scheme Purpose: Facilitate import of capital goods at zero or concessional customs duty for quality export production. Eligibility: Manufacturer exporters, merchant exporters with supporting manufacturers, and service providers. Requires valid IEC. Benefits: Concessional duty in exchange for fulfilling export obligations. Application: Online via DGFT portal (https://www.dgft.gov.in/) with DSC and IEC.

Coir Vikas Yojana (CVY) Purpose: Promote development and modernization of the coir industry. Eligibility: Coir units, individuals, SHGs, and cooperatives. Mahila Coir Yojana component targets women. Benefits: Financial assistance for modernization, skill development, and setting up new units. Application: Apply through Coir Board (https://coirboard.gov.in/) or myScheme portal.

Public Procurement Policy for MSEs Purpose: Mandates Central Ministries/CPSEs to procure at least 25% of annual requirements from MSEs. Eligibility: MSEs registered with Udyam, NSIC, or KVIC. Benefits: Free tender sets, EMD exemption, and purchase preference within L1+15% price band. Application: No separate application; provide valid Udyam registration details when participating in tenders.
